meta_title: For Students: passive income to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to passive income and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Passive Income to Solve 'Can't Finish Projects'
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'can't finish projects' cycle. Learn to build passive income AI apps using Antigravity and Claude Code. A masterclass for student developers.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is excessive scope, not lack of skill. By focusing on micro-SaaS apps that generate passive income, you align your technical efforts with market value. Use AI agents like Claude Code to automate the boilerplate, allowing you to ship small, functional projects in hours, not weeks.
content.intro:
You sit down at 2:00 AM, the glow of the IDE reflecting in your eyes, ready to build the next unicorn. By 4:00 AM, the initial excitement has dissolved into a graveyard of half-baked repos and broken dependencies. The 'can't finish projects' syndrome isn't a lack of talent; it is a design flaw in how you approach your development pipeline.

True mastery isn't found in infinite tutorials, but in the friction of shipping. By pivoting your focus toward building small, passive income-generating tools, you transform your portfolio from a list of 'in-progress' experiments into a collection of live, revenue-producing assets. This is how you stop dreaming and start executing.

steps.title: Define the Micro-Problem
steps.description: Identify a singular, painful workflow problem that people will pay $5/month to solve. Focus on a specific niche to avoid scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a product strategist. Brainstorm 5 micro-SaaS ideas that solve specific, boring business problems for freelance creators. Keep the scope limited to a single feature set.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project requirements document for a micro-SaaS that automates email scheduling for small businesses.'

steps.title: Scaffold with Antigravity
steps.description: Initialize your project structure. Don't waste time on folder configurations; use AI to set up your production-ready environment immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular Next.js 14 architecture with Supabase integration. Include a directory structure for API routes and shared UI components.

steps.title: Automate the Backend Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write your business logic. Focus on the core value proposition: the 'Aha!' moment for your user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure serverless function that integrates with the Stripe API to handle one-time subscription payments for a SaaS product.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create a Stripe webhook handler to update user subscription status in the Supabase users table.'

steps.title: Rapid UI Implementation
steps.description: Build the interface using pre-built components. Speed is your primary metric here; don't reinvent the wheel.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive landing page using Tailwind CSS that emphasizes a clear Call to Action and a simple pricing table.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Refactor the hero section to include a high-converting conversion form and clean typography.'

steps.title: Deploy and Iterate
steps.description: Move your code to production. Feedback from one real user is worth more than 100 hours of hypothetical coding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a checklist for deploying a Next.js application to Vercel, including environment variable management and domain configuration.

pro_tips: Never build a login system from scratch; use Clerk or Supabase Auth to save 20 hours of security implementation.
The '10-Hour Rule': If a project takes more than 10 hours to reach a MVP state, you are over-engineering it.
Market before build: Post your landing page on X or Reddit before writing the backend code to validate interest.
Use AI for documentation: Have Claude Code generate your README.md based on your commit history to keep your repository professional.
Focus on 'boring' problems: The most successful passive income apps solve mundane tasks for businesses, not novel consumer apps.
content.next_steps: Once your first app is live, spend 30 minutes a day on 'growth hacking'—finding your first 5 users on platforms like IndieHackers or Twitter. Do not start a second project until you have at least one paying user.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to stop the cycle of unfinished projects and start building a real, revenue-generating portfolio. The only difference between a student and a founder is the decision to ship. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
